title: Claude Mastery Weekend
description: A 2-day structured program to go from basic Claude user to full AI-powered workflow in one weekend.

modules.title: Claude Chat Basics (Saturday Morning)
modules.agentInstructions: Get Claude set up properly and learn the one prompt framework that replaces everything else.

steps.title: Install and Configure Claude
steps.content: Go to claude.com/download and install the desktop app. Pay for the Pro plan ($20/month). Once inside, select **Opus 4.6 + Extended Thinking** as your model.

steps.title: Connect Your Tools
steps.content: Navigate to **Connectors** and link your Slack, Google Drive, and Notion accounts. This lets Claude read and work across your existing workflow.

steps.title: Master the One Prompt Framework
steps.content:
Stop writing long prompts. Use this instead:

> *"I want to [TASK] for [SUCCESS CRITERIA]. Use AskUserQuestion before you start."*

The `AskUserQuestion` instruction forces Claude to gather context before acting. This single habit separates basic users from power users.

**Reference:** claude.ai/basics

modules.title: Claude Cowork Setup (Saturday Afternoon)
modules.agentInstructions: Replace prompting forever with a personal context folder Claude always reads first.

steps.title: Create Your Folder Structure
steps.content:
Open **Cowork** and create a root folder called `Claude-Cowork`. Inside it, create four subfolders:

- `About Me`
- `Templates`
- `Projects`
- `Outputs`

steps.title: Build Your Context Files
steps.content:
Create two markdown files:

- **about-me.md** — What you do, how you work, your goals, your role
- **anti-ai-style.md** — Words and phrases you would never say. This kills generic AI tone instantly.

steps.title: Set Global Instructions
steps.content:
Go to **Settings → Cowork → Edit** and paste:

> *"Always read my files first, never edit my originals, deliver everything to CLAUDE OUTPUTS."*

Your prompt is now two lines plus your folder. Prompting is no longer the bottleneck.

**Reference:** claude-co.work

modules.title: Skills and Plugins (Sunday Morning)
modules.agentInstructions: Build reusable automations for your most repeated tasks so Claude executes them without being asked.

steps.title: Build Your First Skill
steps.content:
Open Cowork and type:

> *"Use the skill-creator to help me build a skill for [your most repeated task]."*

Claude will interview you. Answer specifically. It will generate a **SKILL.md** file.

steps.title: Test and Refine the Skill
steps.content:
Ask Claude: *"When would you use this skill?"*

If the answer is vague, rewrite the description until it is precise. A good skill fires automatically in the right context. A vague skill never fires at all.

steps.title: Upload and Install
steps.content: Go to **Settings → Capabilities → Skills → Upload** and add your SKILL.md file. It now runs automatically — no slash command needed.

steps.title: Install Plugins
steps.content:
Go to **Cowork → Customize → Browse Plugins** and install plugins relevant to your workflow. Your skill handles the process. Your about-me.md handles the tone. Two layers running simultaneously.

**Reference:** Skills setup guide linked in original post.

modules.title: Code with Claude (Sunday Afternoon — Part 1)
modules.agentInstructions: Let Claude write, manage, and remember your code projects end to end.

steps.title: Connect GitHub
steps.content: Click the **Code tab** in Claude. Create a folder. Connect your GitHub account (free) via **Settings → Connectors**.

steps.title: Prompt for Full Project Creation
steps.content:
Use this prompt:

> *"Create a GitHub repo named [project]. Code everything. Don't ask for permissions."*

Removing permission gates is what makes Claude move fast.

steps.title: Set Up VS Code Integration
steps.content: Download **VS Code** and install the Claude extension. Turn on **Skip Permissions** inside the extension settings. This is the switch that takes you to 100x speed.

steps.title: Create a Project Memory File
steps.content:
At the end of every session paste this:

> *"Create a CLAUDE.md file with everything you learned about this project."*

Claude now remembers your fonts, colors, structure, and decisions permanently. No re-explaining next session.

**Reference:** Claude code guide linked in original post.

modules.title: Claude Computer Use (Sunday Afternoon — Part 2)
modules.agentInstructions: Stop working inside Claude. Let Claude work on your computer.

steps.title: Enable Computer Use
steps.content:
Go to **Settings → Desktop App** and turn on:

- **Browser Use**
- **Computer Use**

Claude can now see and interact with your screen.

steps.title: Connect Your Phone with Dispatch
steps.content: Install **Dispatch** and connect your phone. You can now text Claude a task from anywhere and it executes on your desktop.

steps.title: Schedule Recurring Tasks
steps.content:
Go to the **left sidebar → Scheduled**. Write your prompt. Pick a frequency. Claude now runs tasks on your computer without you touching it.

This is the final level. You are no longer working inside Claude. Claude is working inside your computer.

**Reference:** Claude Computer guide linked in original post.

modules.title: The Progression Summary
modules.agentInstructions: A quick map of what you have built and where most people get stuck.

steps.title: Understand the Levels
steps.content:
| Level | What You Built | Where Most People Stop |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Basic Claude Chat | Most people stay here |
| 2 | Cowork + Context Files | You killed prompting |
| 3 | Skills + Plugins | Claude works automatically |
| 4 | Code + Computer Use | Claude works for you |
verificationQuestions: Which level do most people stop at, and what do they miss?
What is the key difference between Level 2 and Level 3?

steps.title: Your Next Action
steps.content:
Pick the highest level you completed. Identify one real task from your work this week. Run it fully through that level before moving to the next.

Depth beats speed. One workflow mastered beats five workflows started.

*Framework by **Ruben Hassid** — [linkedin.com/in/ruben-hassid](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ruben-hassid/)*
